mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic output expansion, which drives corporate revenue and earnings expectations upward, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 52% of observations following month-over-month industrial production increases, or the Rank IC between INDPRO changes and returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% represents restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs beyond the threshold that supports equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of instances when the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, or the mean forward return during these periods is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the unemployment rate and declines in industrial production jointly signal a recessionary contraction in both labor markets and real output, which compresses aggregate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2pp over 3 months and INDPRO declines over same period
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances when UNRATE rises and INDPRO falls simultaneously, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a joint model.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, declines in housing starts signal that higher mortgage rates are suppressing residential investment and construction employment, which reduces aggregate demand and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines during monetary tightening cycles are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 50% of observations, or the signal rank correlation is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate that rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low signals a deterioration in labor market conditions that precedes weaker consumer spending and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of trigger events where UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its 12-month low, or the mean return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Positive nominal retail sales growth combined with subdued inflation indicates real demand expansion without concurrent margin pressure from input costs, which supports revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month change below 1.0%
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when RSAFS grows while CPIAUCSL remains subdued, or the interaction term fails to outperform single-factor models.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-recession recovery periods signals rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following industrial production acceleration during post-recession recovery are negative in greater than 50% of observations, or Rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and output, which boosts economy-wide corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly real GDP growth surprises are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in greater than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC between GDP surprises and returns is negative.
